stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a significant open-source LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The tags confirm its relevance to major AI frameworks like transformers and the Llama family.

stories.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple has integrated end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging support into the Messages app on iOS.
This feature allows iPhone users to have encrypted chats with Android users.
The update, part of iOS 26.5, ensures that neither Apple nor Google can view the message content.

stories.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google successfully identified and neutralized a zero-day exploit.
The exploit was reportedly developed using Artificial Intelligence by sophisticated threat actors.
The attack aimed for a 'mass exploitation event' capable of bypassing two-factor authentication.

stories.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
The core of the dispute is the accusation that OpenAI is prioritizing profit over its goal of benefiting humanity.
The high-stakes court battle could significantly impact the future direction and operations of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The study introduces the 'memory curse,' finding that expanding LLM context windows systematically degrades cooperation in multi-agent social dilemmas.
Testing across 7 LLMs and 4 games, the degradation in cooperation was observed in 18 out of 28 model-game settings.
The research isolates the mechanism, linking the breakdown to the erosion of forward-looking intent within the LLMs' reasoning traces.

stories.title: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
stories.author: Vincent Conitzer
bullet_points: The research identifies a 'memory curse' where expanding LLM context windows degrades cooperative intent in multi-agent systems.
The study tested 7 LLMs across 4 games over 500 rounds, finding that expanded history negatively impacted cooperation in 18 of 28 settings.
The mechanism involves lexical analysis showing that the breakdown correlates with eroding forward-looking intent in the models' reasoning traces.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
Key technologies mentioned include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Matter and OpenADR team up to connect smart homes to the grid
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Matter (smart home standard) and OpenADR (grid communication protocol) have partnered.
The collaboration aims to simplify the integration of smart home devices with the electrical grid.
This integration will facilitate easier participation of connected appliances in Demand Response (DR) programs.

stories.title: Rubric-Grounded RL: Structured Judge Rewards for Generalizable Reasoning
stories.author: Dan O'Malley
bullet_points: Introduces 'rubric-grounded reinforcement learning' (RL) to improve AI reasoning.
The method uses a frozen LLM judge to provide structured, multi-criterion rewards instead of binary outcomes.
This approach offers a partial-credit optimization signal, allowing models to be graded along specific task criteria.

stories.title: Tool Calling is Linearly Readable and Steerable in Language Models
stories.author: Maria Perez-Ortiz
bullet_points: The research demonstrates that the tool selection process within language models (like Gemma, Qwen, and Llama) is linearly readable and steerable.
By manipulating the difference in internal activations between two tools, researchers can reliably force a model to choose a specific tool with high accuracy.
This work addresses the 'invisible failure' problem in tool-calling agents, offering a mechanism to probe and control model decision-making.

stories.title: GLiGuard: Schema-Conditioned Classification for LLM Safeguard
stories.author: Ash Lewis
bullet_points: Introduces GLiGuard, a novel, highly efficient safeguard model for Large Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the latency and scaling issues of current guardrail models, which unnecessarily use large autoregressive decoders for classification.
GLiGuard achieves safety compliance using a significantly smaller 0.3B parameter model, making real-time, multi-aspect moderation more feasible.

stories.title: CUDA-oxide: Nvidia's official Rust to CUDA compiler
stories.author: adamnemecek
bullet_points: Nvidia has released CUDA-oxide, an official compiler enabling Rust to target CUDA.
This represents a significant step in integrating Rust into the high-performance computing ecosystem.
It lowers the barrier for Rust developers to leverage Nvidia's GPU acceleration capabilities.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within open-source model environments.
It bridges the gap between proprietary models (Anthropic) and local/open-source tooling (Ollama).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of open models with OpenAI's Codex CLI.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives (e.g., gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b) instead of proprietary models.

stories.title: Texas sues Netflix for advertising ‘bait and switch’ and spying
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has sued Netflix.
The lawsuit alleges Netflix engaged in 'bait and switch' regarding its ad-free promise.
The complaint also accuses Netflix of compromising user data to the ad tech industry.

stories.title: GM settles California lawsuit claiming it sold driving habit data to insurance companies
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: GM settled a California lawsuit regarding the sale of driver location and habit data.
The settlement requires GM to cease selling customer information to data brokers for five years.
The financial penalty associated with the settlement is $12.75 million.

stories.title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Starlink has disabled a specific GPS-like location feature.
This action is unlikely to stop research into alternative location tracking methods.
The story highlights ongoing interest in GPS alternatives despite corporate restrictions.

stories.title: Sony's failed war against Internet piracy may doom other copyright lawsuits
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A Supreme Court ruling involving cable firm Cox could significantly impact copyright lawsuits.
The ruling may benefit various tech providers, extending beyond just Internet Service Providers (ISPs).
The case relates to Sony's past efforts against internet piracy, suggesting a potential shift in legal precedent.

stories.title: EmambaIR: Efficient Visual State Space Model for Event-guided Image Reconstruction
stories.author: Yunhang Qian
bullet_points: EmambaIR introduces a novel Visual State Space Model (VSSM) for event-guided image reconstruction.
It addresses limitations in existing methods, specifically the global correlation issues of CNNs and the quadratic complexity of ViTs.
The model aims to provide an efficient alternative for high-resolution image reconstruction using event-based data.

stories.title: Flow-OPD: On-Policy Distillation for Flow Matching Models
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Introduces Flow-OPD, a novel approach applying On-Policy Distillation (OPD) to Flow Matching (FM) models.
Addresses critical bottlenecks in FM text-to-image models, specifically reward sparsity and gradient interference during multi-task alignment.
Aims to mitigate the 'seesaw effect' and reward hacking issues common when jointly optimizing heterogeneous objectives in generative models.

stories.title: CA-SQL: Complexity-Aware Inference Time Reasoning for Text-to-SQL via Exploration and Compute Budget Allocation
stories.author: Nianwen Xue
bullet_points: CA-SQL is a new Text-to-SQL pipeline designed to improve performance on challenging tasks in the Bird-Bench (BIRD) benchmark.
The core problem addressed is inadequate solution space exploration during inference-time reasoning in LLMs for Text-to-SQL.
The method utilizes complexity-aware inference time reasoning and compute budget allocation to find promising candidate queries.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: The Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) aims to overcome the slow generation speed of byte-level language models.
BLT utilizes a new training technique called BLT Diffusion (BLT-D) by incorporating an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.
The research addresses the performance gap between byte-level and token-level models while improving generation efficiency.

stories.title: SCOPE: Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration for Complex Image Generation
stories.author: Feng Zhao
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of faithfully realizing complex visual intents in text-to-image models.
Introduces the concept of 'semantic commitments' that must be tracked across grounding, generation, and verification.
Formalizes the 'Conceptual Rift,' describing the failure to maintain identity of requirements throughout the generation lifecycle.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which relies on pairwise comparisons for alignment.
It proposes a method to leverage richer preference structures present when training data includes multiple rollouts per prompt.
The current pairwise DPO approach discards valuable information, potentially leading to unstable model training.

stories.title: Normalizing Trajectory Models
stories.author: Josh Susskind
bullet_points: NTM addresses limitations in few-step diffusion models, where coarse transitions break the standard Gaussian denoising assumption.
The proposed Normalizing Trajectory Models (NTM) model each reverse step using an expressive conditional normalizing flow.
A key advantage of NTM is that it allows for exact likelihood training, unlike existing distillation or consistency-based methods.

stories.title: Zero-Shot Imagined Speech Decoding via Imagined-to-Listened MEG Mapping
stories.author: Shihab Shamma
bullet_points: Proposes a novel method to decode imagined speech using non-invasive MEG recordings.
Addresses the scarcity and alignment challenges of imagined speech datasets by leveraging listening data.
Utilizes paired listened and imagined MEG recordings from trained musicians for improved performance.

stories.title: GRAPHLCP: Structure-Aware Localized Conformal Prediction on Graphs
stories.author: Sourav Medya
bullet_points: GRAPHLCP addresses the challenge of applying Conformal Prediction (CP) to Graph Neural Networks (GNNs).
The method aims to overcome limitations in existing CP approaches on graphs, which suffer from combinatorial complexity and unreliable embedding-space proximity.
GRAPHLCP introduces a structure-aware, localized conformal prediction technique for better uncertainty quantification on graph data.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: Addresses the slow autoregressive generation bottleneck in byte-level language models (LMs).
Introduces the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) with new training and generation techniques.
Features BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a fast variant trained using an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.

stories.title: Beyond Pairs: Your Language Model is Secretly Optimizing a Preference Graph
stories.author: Shervin Malmasi
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which relies on pairwise comparisons for aligning language models.
It proposes a method to leverage richer preference structures present when training data includes multiple rollouts per prompt, which standard DPO ignores.
The proposed approach aims to overcome issues like discarding transitivity and introducing conflicting supervision inherent in collapsing multi-rollout data into simple pairs.

stories.title: Don't Get Your Kroneckers in a Twist: Gaussian Processes on High-Dimensional Incomplete Grids
stories.author: Ove Christiansen
bullet_points: Introduces CUTS-GPR, a novel method for numerically exact Gaussian Process Regression (GPR).
Addresses the challenge of high-dimensional data by achieving near-linear scaling with training data ($N$).
Leverages an additive kernel and incomplete grid structure to enable fast kernel matrix-vector products.

stories.title: PropSplat: Map-Free RF Field Reconstruction via 3D Gaussian Propagation Splatting
stories.author: Joseph Camp
bullet_points: PropSplat introduces a map-free method for reconstructing Radio Frequency (RF) fields.
It utilizes 3D anisotropic Gaussian primitives to model propagation, bypassing the need for detailed 3D maps or extensive measurements.
This approach is positioned as a solution for rapid deployments where geographic data is scarce or outdated.

stories.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
stories.author: Heng Huang
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of hand-crafted Test-Time Scaling (TTS) methods for improving LLM performance.
It introduces AutoTTS, an environment-driven framework designed to automate the allocation of computation during inference.
The core contribution shifts the research focus from designing individual TTS heuristics to defining comprehensive environments.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Structured Data Extraction from Full CMR Reports via Distilled LLMs
stories.author: Yuan Xue
bullet_points: Introduces CMR-EXTR, a framework for converting unstructured Cardiac MRI (CMR) reports into structured, auditable data.
Employs a teacher-student distillation pipeline to enable lightweight, fully offline inference using distilled LLMs.
A key feature is the assignment of per-field confidence scores to facilitate quality control in clinical data extraction.

stories.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
stories.author: Srinivasan Iyer
bullet_points: The Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) aims to overcome the slow generation speed of byte-level language models.
BLT introduces BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a new variant trained with an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective.
This work seeks to match token-level model performance while avoiding subword vocabularies, addressing a key limitation in byte-level LMs.

stories.title: Position: Mechanistic Interpretability Must Disclose Identification Assumptions for Causal Claims
stories.author: Fengming Liu
bullet_points: Mechanistic interpretability papers are increasingly using causal language (e.g., circuits, mediators).
A review of 10 papers found a lack of explicit identification assumptions for these causal claims.
Validation metrics (like faithfulness or ablation effects) are often presented as causal support without stating underlying assumptions.

stories.title: How to Secure AI Agents in Production: What MCP Gets Right (and What It Doesn’t)
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: Focuses on the practical security challenges of deploying AI agents in production environments.
Addresses the risks associated with granting AI agents access to external tools.
Analyzes best practices (or failures) demonstrated by a specific case study (MCP).

stories.title: Building Team A: An AI System for Turning Volunteer Chaos into Structured Engineering Work
stories.author: miry
bullet_points: Presents an AI-driven workflow system designed to manage volunteer engineering teams.
The system converts informal requests into structured, actionable engineering tasks.
It aims to reduce onboarding friction using multi-agent AI support.

stories.title: Cómo Guiar Asistentes de IA para Construir Agentes Listos para Producción: 8 Patrones Esenciales
stories.author: elizabethfuentes12
bullet_points: The article focuses on practical guidance for directing AI assistants (like ChatGPT, Claude Code, or AWS Kiro).
It outlines 8 essential patterns necessary for building production-ready AI agents.
The content targets developers interested in advanced prompt engineering and AI agent construction.

stories.title: Reddit Starts Blocking the Mobile Web: The "App-Wall" Is Officially Here
stories.author: its_vrushal
bullet_points: Reddit is implementing changes that restrict access to its mobile web experience.
This shift is described as an 'App-Wall,' pushing users toward dedicated mobile applications.
The change impacts how users access content, such as troubleshooting advice or reviews, on mobile devices.
